title: Hitchenia roscoeana

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hitchenia roscoeana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions.
